prompt: Paper-collage stop-motion assembly. Use Image 1 as the exact empty first frame (a flat dark-green paper field) and Image 2 as the exact completed last frame. One continuous locked-off vertical shot, no camera movement, PACED EVENLY ACROSS THE ENTIRE FIVE SECONDS: pieces keep arriving continuously through the whole shot, do NOT finish early and do NOT hold a long static frame. Open on the empty dark-green field; the halftone strongbox slides in and opens, chat-conversation papers fold and drop into it one after another, the brick-red padlock snaps shut on the front of the box, then a round riveted provider window fades in on the right and fills with scrambled shredded-paper ciphertext strips in the last second to complete Image 2. Preserve the 9:16 framing, cream keylines, brick-red cardstock accents, uncoated paper grain, halftone dots, crisp cut edges and soft shadows. Restrained tactile 2D paper craft only. No scene cuts, no camera movement, no zoom, no morphing, no new objects, no text, no letters, no numbers, no logos, no watermark, no UI, no sound.
